name: What happened in that New York case where AI chats got used against someone in court?
description: Court held AI chats (Claude) not privileged in US v. Heppner (NY 2026); discoverable and used against defendant.[1]

mainEntity.name: What happened in that New York case where AI chats got used against someone in court?

acceptedAnswer.text: Court held AI chats (Claude) not privileged in US v. Heppner (NY 2026); discoverable and used against defendant.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: In United States v. Heppner (S.D.N.Y. 2026), the court ruled that defendant's private conversations with Claude AI were not privileged and could be used as evidence, rejecting attorney-client or work product protections.[1] The AI disclaimed legal advice, and no lawyer directed the use, making records discoverable like any document.[1] Key factors: AI cannot form attorney-client relationships, device seizures reveal chats, inconsistencies harm cases.[1][2] Preserve devices carefully, never input case details into consumer AI, and only use AI under direct lawyer supervision for potential protection.[1] Seek counsel experienced in digital evidence.
